examples.translation: People without experience often make the most noise in group discussions despite lacking substance.

examples.sentence: Dalam rapat itu, dia hanya bicara banyak tapi tidak memberikan solusi, benar-benar tong kosong nyaring bunyinya.
examples.translation: In that meeting, he talked a lot but gave no solutions, truly an empty vessel making the most noise.

examples.sentence: Jangan terlalu percaya pada mereka yang tong kosong nyaring bunyinya karena biasanya tidak tahu apa-apa.
examples.translation: Do not trust those who make the most noise but have no substance because they usually know nothing.

definitions: To close a physical book by shutting its covers.

examples.sentence: Setelah selesai membaca, dia dengan hati-hati menutup buku dan meletakkannya di meja belajar.
examples.translation: After finishing reading, he carefully closed the book and placed it on the study table.

examples.sentence: Guru meminta murid untuk tutup buku saat pelajaran selesai agar kelas bisa segera berpindah ke kegiatan lain.
examples.translation: The teacher asked the students to close their books when the lesson was over so the class could move to another activity.

examples.sentence: Dia menutup buku dengan perlahan supaya tidak merusak halaman-halamannya yang sudah tua.
examples.translation: He closed the book slowly so as not to damage its old pages.

examples.sentence: Saat jam pelajaran berakhir, semua siswa serempak menutup buku mereka dan bersiap untuk pulang.
examples.translation: When the lesson time ended, all students simultaneously closed their books and prepared to go home.

definitions: To close the books; to cease recording transactions and finalize accounting records, typically at the end of a period.

examples.sentence: Akhir tahun tiba, perusahaan segera tutup buku untuk menyelesaikan laporan keuangan tahunan.
examples.translation: The end of the year came, and the company immediately closed the books to complete the annual financial report.

examples.sentence: Setelah audit selesai, staf akuntansi menutup buku bulan ini agar neraca dapat disusun dengan tepat.
examples.translation: After the audit was completed, accounting staff closed the books for this month so the balance sheet could be prepared accurately.

examples.sentence: Manajer keuangan memutuskan untuk tutup buku lebih awal karena adanya perubahan dalam sistem pembukuan.
examples.translation: The finance manager decided to close the books earlier due to changes in the bookkeeping system.

definitions: To bring an issue to an end or to consider a matter concluded; to stop further discussion, investigation, or action on a topic.

examples.sentence: Saya rasa kita harus tutup buku tentang masalah lama itu agar bisa fokus pada hal yang lebih penting sekarang.
examples.translation: I think we should close the chapter on that old issue so we can focus on more important matters now.

examples.sentence: Setelah penyelidikan selesai, polisi memutuskan untuk tutup buku kasus tersebut karena kurang bukti.
examples.translation: After the investigation was completed, the police decided to close the case due to lack of evidence.

examples.sentence: Dalam rapat terakhir, pimpinan menginstruksikan untuk tutup buku mengenai konflik internal dan melanjutkan kerja sama.
examples.translation: In the last meeting, the leader instructed to close the book on internal conflicts and continue cooperation.

definitions: To cover or close something physically, often intensively or repeatedly.

examples.sentence: Dia selalu tutup-tutupi luka kecil di tangannya dengan plester agar tidak terlihat kotor.
examples.translation: She always covers small wounds on her hands with a plaster so they don't look dirty.

examples.sentence: Setelah hujan deras, para petani tutup-tutupi tanaman muda mereka dengan plastik agar tidak rusak.
examples.translation: After heavy rain, the farmers covered their young plants with plastic to protect them from damage.

examples.sentence: Anak kecil itu suka tutup-tutupi mainannya dengan selimut saat bermain petak umpet di rumah.
examples.translation: The little child likes to cover his toys with a blanket when playing hide and seek at home.

examples.sentence: Kami tutup-tutupi dokumen penting itu dengan kain agar tidak terkena debu selama renovasi kantor.
examples.translation: We covered the important documents with cloth so they wouldn't get dusty during the office renovation.

definitions: To conceal, hide, or disguise information, facts, or wrongdoing; to cover up.

examples.sentence: Perusahaan mencoba tutup-tutupi kasus penyelewengan dana agar tidak merusak reputasi mereka.
examples.translation: The company tried to cover up the embezzlement case to avoid damaging their reputation.

examples.sentence: Jangan tutup-tutupi kesalahanmu karena itu hanya akan memperburuk keadaan di kemudian hari.
examples.translation: Don't cover up your mistakes because it will only make things worse later.

examples.sentence: Pemerintah dituduh tutup-tutupi fakta terkait insiden kebakaran pabrik yang menelan korban jiwa.
examples.translation: The government was accused of covering up facts related to the factory fire incident that caused casualties.
